text: I hate to be the one to say it but the
universe doesn't actually look like this
or this or this even no one is going to
argue with you that space photography
has come a long way in the last 80 years
the first photo ever taken in space was
this in 1946 at 65 Mi up just above the
beginning of outer space now we get
pictures like this almost monthly it
seems showing us these beautiful
colorful images of the universe around
us but there's one problem when
satellites like the James web telescope
are taking these pictures they're
capturing light across different
wavelengths including infrared
ultraviolet or x-ray which aren't
visible to the human eye NASA will then
go in assigning color to these different
wavelengths to highlight specific
features and structures for the viewer
this process known as false coloring
allows them to reveal details in space
that would otherwise be invisible if you
were actually up there
